About Hunter Holdings

A modern quantitative partner built at the intersection of financial engineering and applied data science.

Hunter Holdings is founded on the belief that advanced analytics should not be reserved for only the largest funds and institutions. We bring the discipline of quantitative research, derivatives thinking, machine learning, and structured decision systems to clients who want clearer signals and stronger financial outcomes.

The firm is led by Dalitso Nthonyiwa, a Master of Financial Engineering candidate with experience across derivative pricing, credit risk, regime-based strategy research, neural-network forecasting, and macro-financial analysis.

What clients gain

  • Sharper capital allocation decisions through model-driven insight
  • Faster understanding of risk exposures and market behavior
  • Decision systems that scale beyond ad hoc spreadsheets
  • Technical credibility for investor, lender, and partner discussions
  • Custom analytics designed around the actual business problem
Built for serious financial work

From market research to risk architecture, we design systems that support higher-conviction decisions.

Services

Capabilities aligned to investment, risk, and growth decisions

Quantitative Research & Strategy Development

Design of systematic strategies, factor studies, regime models, backtests, and performance attribution for discretionary or rules-based investment processes.

Derivative Pricing & Structured Risk Analytics

Implementation of Black–Scholes, Merton, Heston, Bates, and Monte Carlo frameworks to support valuation, sensitivity analysis, and hedging decisions.

Credit Risk Modeling

Probability of default estimation, score bucketing, classification pipelines, and portfolio-level risk segmentation for lenders and financial institutions.

Time-Series Forecasting

Forecasting frameworks for commodities, FX, equities, macro variables, and operational metrics using econometrics and machine learning.

Portfolio & Market Analytics

Volatility studies, PCA, factor decomposition, scenario testing, and market diagnostics that improve allocation decisions and investor reporting.

Data Science for Business Operations

Applied analytics, predictive models, dashboards, and decision-support tools for finance teams, agribusiness, fintech, and research-led organizations.

Why this matters

Value is created when financial theory is translated into usable decisions.

Many organizations have data, but not a robust decision layer. Hunter Holdings turns raw market, lending, or operational data into frameworks that help clients answer difficult questions: What is the fair value? Where is the risk concentrated? Which signals matter? What should be automated next?

Revenue impactbetter entry, allocation, pricing, and product decisions
Risk reductionmore rigorous measurement of downside, volatility, and credit exposure
Operational leveragereusable models and analytics workflows instead of one-off manual analysis
Strategic clarityinvestor-grade narratives supported by quantitative evidence
Selected project themes

Representative work informing the Hunter Holdings offering

Trading Research

Regime-Switching Strategy Research

Modeled market states to evaluate strategy behavior across changing regimes and compare cumulative performance against passive alternatives.

Machine Learning

Gold Return Prediction with Neural Networks

Built and evaluated predictive models for gold returns using historical market features and neural-network architectures.

Risk & Credit

Loan Default Classification & PD Estimation

Developed classification systems and score segmentation logic to estimate default risk and improve credit decisioning.

Portfolio Analytics

PCA on Multi-Asset Portfolios

Reduced dimensionality to identify key risk drivers and understand co-movement across assets.

Derivatives

Derivative Pricing Framework

Implemented closed-form and simulation-based models for European options under stochastic volatility and jump-diffusion assumptions.

Economic Intelligence

Banking & Macro-Financial Analysis

Analyzed financial and economic datasets to surface trends, volatility patterns, and business-relevant indicators.

Engagement model

Flexible ways to work together

Advisory Sprint 2–4 weeks • focused model design, analytics audit, or strategic diagnosis
Build Project 4–10 weeks • end-to-end development of a model, pricing engine, or dashboard
Research Retainer monthly • recurring quant support for strategy, reporting, or risk review
Custom Institutional Work scoped engagements for funds, lenders, and specialist teams
